head.example: My head sits on top of my neck.
head.function: Your head holds your brain, eyes, ears, nose, and mouth.

hair.example: My hair keeps my head warm and looks nice.
hair.function: Hair protects your scalp and helps you look beautiful.

eye.example: I see the world with my two eyes!
eye.function: Eyes let you see colors, shapes, and everything around you.

ear.example: My ears help me hear music and my friends talking.
ear.function: Ears catch sound waves so you can hear.

nose.example: I smell flowers and pizza with my nose.
nose.function: Your nose helps you smell and breathe.

mouth.example: I talk, eat, and smile with my mouth.
mouth.function: Your mouth is for eating, speaking, and breathing.

tooth.example: I brush my teeth every morning and night.
tooth.function: Teeth help you chew food into small pieces.

tongue.example: My tongue tastes sweet, sour, salty, and bitter flavors.
tongue.function: Your tongue helps you taste food and speak.

neck.example: My neck connects my head to my body.
neck.function: Your neck holds your head and lets you look around.

shoulder.example: I carry my backpack on my shoulders.
shoulder.function: Shoulders connect your arms to your chest.

arm.example: I lift things with my strong arms.
arm.function: Arms let you reach, grab, lift, and throw.

elbow.example: My elbow bends so I can bring food to my mouth.
elbow.function: Your elbow lets your arm bend in the middle.

hand.example: I write, draw, and clap with my hands.
hand.function: Hands have fingers to grab and hold things.

finger.example: I have ten fingers, including two thumbs!
finger.function: Fingers help you pick up small things.

thumb.example: My thumb is special because it can touch my other fingers.
thumb.function: Your thumb helps you grip and hold objects.

chest.example: My chest holds my heart and lungs.
chest.function: Your chest protects your heart and lungs inside.

belly.example: My belly growls when I am hungry.
belly.function: Your belly holds your stomach and intestines.

back.example: My back helps me sit up straight and bend over.
back.function: Your back has your spine, which supports your body.

leg.example: I walk, run, and jump with my legs.
leg.function: Legs let you move your body around.

knee.example: My knee bends so I can sit down.
knee.function: Your knee connects your upper and lower leg.

foot.example: My feet keep me balanced when I stand.
foot.function: Feet support your weight and help you walk.

toe.example: I have ten little toes, five on each foot.
toe.function: Toes help you balance when you walk.

heart.example: My heart beats faster when I run.
heart.function: Your heart pumps blood all through your body.

lung.example: My lungs fill with air when I breathe in.
lung.function: Lungs bring oxygen into your body.

brain.example: My brain tells my body what to do.
brain.function: Your brain thinks, remembers, and controls everything.

bone.example: My skeleton has 206 bones inside me!
bone.function: Bones give your body shape and support.

skin.example: My skin covers my whole body like a blanket.
skin.function: Skin protects your body from germs and injuries.

muscle.example: My muscles help me lift and move things.
muscle.function: Muscles pull on your bones to make you move.

blood.example: Blood flows through my body inside tubes called veins.
blood.function: Blood carries oxygen and food to all your cells.
